summary: End-to-end testing of 'Captain works under Cashier shift' feature for Maestro EGP. Backend: 10/10 captain-feature tests PASS + 4/4 unassigned-delivery regression tests PASS. Frontend: all data-testids present and wired correctly; captain row shows 12,000 IQD held, orders tab shows seeded order #1004 with 'بحوزة الكابتن' badge. One UX brittleness: collect button uses native window.confirm() which is hard to drive from Playwright (same pattern flagged in iter217 cleanup feature).

minor.issue: Inserts captain_shift_links documents WITHOUT tenant_id field, but /api/captain/my-shift and /api/shifts/available-captains both filter by tenant_id when present. Result: a freshly-seeded link is invisible to the API until a re-link is performed via POST /api/shifts/{shift_id}/link-captain. Fix: add tenant_id='default' to the inserted captain_shift_links document and to the shift document.

Backend rejects uppercase order_type values like 'DINE_IN' with the Arabic delivery-only message (because 'DINE_IN' is not in the allowed ('dine_in','takeaway') set). Recommend either accepting case-insensitive order_type or returning a more accurate 422/'invalid order_type' message instead of the misleading delivery message.

integration_issues.issue: Click handler uses native window.confirm() (CaptainsManagement.js line 55). Playwright cannot easily auto-accept native confirms after the page has rendered; must register page.on('dialog', d=>d.accept()) BEFORE the click. Backend /api/captains/collect itself works correctly (verified via pytest). Recommend switching to shadcn AlertDialog for consistency with the rest of the app and to make UI testing reliable.

issues: Page header, totals cards, captains/orders tabs and table all render correctly with proper RTL layout. No contrast issues observed.

test_results_backend.test_captain_creates_dinein_attributed: PASS (cashier_id, shift_id, captain_id, captain_cash_status='held' all set on order)
test_results_backend.test_captain_delivery_rejected: PASS (400 with Arabic message التوصيل من اختصاص الكاشير)

test_results_frontend.dashboard_tile_navigates: Partial - click attempted but beforeunload warning fired; direct nav to /captains-management works fine and page loads
test_results_frontend.captain_row_text: كابتن أحمد 1 طلب باع 12,000 IQD سلّم 0 IQD متبقٍ معه 12,000 IQD تأكيد الاستلام
test_results_frontend.collect_action_pending_drops_to_zero: INCONCLUSIVE in UI (native window.confirm not auto-accepted in test); backend endpoint verified working in pytest

action_items: Fix /app/backend/seed_captain_test_data.py: add tenant_id='default' to the captain_shift_links insert (and ideally to the shifts insert) so /api/captain/my-shift returns linked=true straight after seeding without needing a manual re-link.

Replace native window.confirm() in /app/frontend/src/pages/CaptainsManagement.js handleCollect (line 55) with a shadcn AlertDialog for consistency with rest of app and to make UI testing reliable. Same recommendation applies to handleCleanupNonCashier in Reports.js from iter217.

(Minor) For POST /api/orders, when a captain submits an order_type not in {'dine_in','takeaway'} (e.g. uppercase 'DINE_IN'), the backend returns the Arabic 'delivery is for cashier only' error which is misleading. Either normalize case (e.g. order.order_type.lower()) before the check or return a clearer message.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is huge (>8000 lines) — create_order alone spans ~270 lines (6090-6360). Captain attribution logic in create_order is tightly coupled to the function; consider extracting a `_resolve_captain_shift_context(user, db)` helper used by both create_order and offline sync paths.
Backend captain order_type check at server.py:6098 uses strict lowercase membership ('dine_in','takeaway'). Be defensive: normalize order_type case OR add a centralized OrderType enum.
Seed script omits tenant_id on captain_shift_links — silent data integrity bug; would also affect any future seed/test consumer.
Frontend CaptainsManagement.js uses window.confirm — inconsistent with shadcn AlertDialog used elsewhere; brittle for automated tests and worse UX in RTL.
/api/captain/my-shift returns full shift document including internal mongo fields — fine since the projection excludes _id, but consider only exposing the subset the captain POS actually needs.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all UI elements verified; collect-action UI not auto-completable due to native confirm)

seed_data_creation: Used /app/backend/seed_captain_test_data.py (with workaround: re-link via API). State at end-of-run: shift is now CLOSED (because test_close_blocked_then_collect_then_close closed it). Re-seed before any further run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend feature is solid — all 10 captain tests + 4 delivery regression tests pass. Only the seed script needs the tenant_id fix and the frontend collect UI should be migrated off native window.confirm. The seeded shift at end of this run is CLOSED; re-run seed_captain_test_data.py before any further testing. To exercise the collect UI from Playwright, register `page.on('dialog', lambda d: asyncio.ensure_future(d.accept()))` BEFORE clicking collect-btn — that should drive the native confirm.
rca of the issue: 1) The 'my-shift returns linked=false right after seed' bug RCA: seed inserts captain_shift_links WITHOUT tenant_id; /api/captain/my-shift queries with {captain_id, active:true, tenant_id:'default'} → no match. Mitigation: add tenant_id to seed inserts. 2) The 'collect-btn UI did not visibly clear pending' RCA: handler uses native window.confirm; Playwright override of window.confirm via page.evaluate did not take effect in time (no /captains/collect network call observed). Mitigation: page.on('dialog', d=>d.accept()) registered BEFORE click, or convert to AlertDialog.
